steps.title: Read the histogram
steps.content: The bars cover length intervals of width 50 starting at 0. Frequencies are: $[0,50)$: 5, $[50,100)$: 3, $[100,150)$: 4, $[150,200)$: 3, $[200,250)$: 5. Total $= 5+3+4+3+5 = 20$, matching the 20 data points.

steps.title: Locate the maximum
steps.content: Because the rightmost nonempty bar is $[200, 250)$ and it contains 5 values, the maximum length in the data set must be at least 200 feet and less than 250 feet.

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A 68 lies in $[50,100)$, far below the last bar — impossible as the max.
  • B 118 lies in $[100,150)$ — still below 200.
  • C 168 lies in $[150,200)$ — still below 200.
  • D 218 lies in $[200,250)$, which is exactly where the maximum must sit.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
